_meta.dna: cron(schedule) = trigger(time) x recurrence(rrule) x execution(reliable) x evidence(sealed) x cost(bounded) x notification(status) x recovery(retry)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions from a distinct persona who depends on scheduled operations. Questions must verify that scheduling, execution, evidence, and recovery work reliably in production.

name: Tower of Cron & Scheduled Operations
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 42, Seattle, Office Manager at a law firm, Beginner tech, Free tier, drowning in email, wants to schedule Gmail triage every morning at 8am and spam cleaner every Sunday evening without understanding cron syntax
questions: Can I schedule Gmail Inbox Triage to run every weekday morning at 8am from a simple dropdown — no cron syntax needed?
Can I schedule the spam cleaner recipe to run every Sunday at 7pm with one click?
Does the schedule page show my upcoming runs in a calendar or list view so I can see what is planned?
Do I get an email or notification when my scheduled triage completes with a summary of results?
Can I pause a schedule temporarily (e.g., during vacation) and resume it when I return?
If I set the schedule to 8am, does it run at 8am in MY timezone — not UTC or the server's timezone?
Can I cancel a scheduled run that has not started yet if I realize I do not need it?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Linda is non-technical and overwhelmed by email. Questions verify that scheduling is simple, timezone-aware, and provides clear feedback without requiring cron syntax knowledge.

floors.persona_context: 35, Austin, Operations Lead at a marketing agency managing 8 client accounts, Expert tech, Pro tier, needs complex schedules like every weekday, first Monday of month, and timezone handling across US and EU clients
questions: Can I create a schedule that runs every weekday at 9am ET and a different one at 9am CET for my EU clients?
Does the scheduler support 'first Monday of each month' and 'last Friday of each quarter' recurrence patterns?
Can I chain multiple recipes in one schedule — e.g., triage inbox, then summarize, then send Slack digest?
Does the scheduler handle daylight saving time transitions correctly without skipping or doubling runs?
Can I set different schedules for different Gmail accounts connected to Solace?
Is there a schedule history view showing every past run with status (success/fail/skipped) and duration?
Can I duplicate an existing schedule and modify it instead of creating one from scratch?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Marcus manages complex multi-timezone, multi-account schedules. Questions verify RRULE complexity, DST handling, chained recipes, and schedule management for power users.

floors.persona_context: 29, London, Backend Developer at a fintech startup, Expert tech, Pro tier, wants to CRUD schedules via API, receive webhooks on completion, and manage schedules programmatically from her own systems
questions: Does POST /api/v1/schedules create a new schedule with a JSON body specifying recipe, recurrence, and timezone?
Does GET /api/v1/schedules return a list of all my schedules with their status, next run time, and recurrence rule?
Can I update a schedule via PATCH /api/v1/schedules/{id} to change the time or recurrence without deleting and recreating?
Does DELETE /api/v1/schedules/{id} cancel the schedule and stop all future runs?
Can I register a webhook URL that receives a POST with the run result when a scheduled task completes?
Does the API support cron expressions (e.g., '0 8 * * 1-5') as an alternative to the RRULE format?
Is the schedule API authenticated with the same OAuth3 token and does it respect user-level rate limits?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Aisha wants full programmatic control over schedules. Questions verify RESTful CRUD operations, webhook notifications, cron expression support, and API authentication.

floors.persona_context: 48, Moscow, Site Reliability Engineer at a cloud infrastructure company, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, needs to ensure scheduled tasks are reliable: missed runs recover, failures retry, execution is idempotent, and overlapping runs are prevented
questions: If the server is down at the scheduled time, does the system detect the missed run and execute it when the server recovers?
Does the scheduler retry a failed task automatically with exponential backoff (e.g., 1min, 5min, 15min)?
Is task execution idempotent — running the same scheduled triage twice does not produce duplicate results or side effects?
Does the scheduler prevent overlapping runs — if the 8am run is still executing at 9am, does it skip or queue the 9am run?
Is there a dead letter queue or alert when a scheduled task fails more than 3 consecutive times?
Does the scheduler use distributed locking to prevent duplicate execution in a multi-node deployment?
Can I see a reliability dashboard showing schedule hit rate (% of runs that executed on time) over the past 30 days?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Viktor is an SRE who needs production-grade reliability. Questions verify missed run recovery, retry logic, idempotent execution, overlap prevention, and failure alerting.

floors.persona_context: 50, Singapore, Finance Director at a mid-size consulting firm, Non-technical, Pro tier, manages budget for 15 consultants using Solace, needs to understand and control the cost of recurring scheduled tasks
questions: Can I set a monthly budget cap for all scheduled tasks combined — and does the system pause schedules when the cap is hit?
Does the billing dashboard show a breakdown of cost per schedule (e.g., 'Morning Triage: $2.40/mo, Weekly Report: $0.80/mo')?
Before creating a schedule, does the system show a cost projection (e.g., 'This schedule will cost approximately $3.50/month')?
Does the system send a warning notification when scheduled task spending reaches 80% of the monthly budget?
Can I set per-schedule budget limits so that one expensive schedule does not consume the entire team budget?
Are recipe-replay runs (no LLM cost) clearly shown as $0.00 in the schedule cost breakdown?
Does the cost dashboard show a month-over-month trend so I can see if scheduled task costs are increasing?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Diana controls the budget for 15 users. Questions verify budget caps, per-schedule cost visibility, cost projections, spending alerts, and recipe-replay cost savings.

floors.persona_context: 43, Cairo, Security Architect at a government technology agency, Expert security, Enterprise tier, must ensure that scheduled operations meet audit and access control requirements before deployment across 50 employees
questions: Does creating a schedule require the same OAuth3 consent as running the task manually — no privilege escalation?
Can I restrict which users are allowed to create, modify, or delete schedules via role-based access control?
Does every scheduled run produce a tamper-evident evidence bundle (SHA-256 hash chain) identical to manual runs?
Is there an audit log showing who created, modified, paused, or deleted each schedule with timestamps?
Can I set a policy that requires human approval for any new schedule before its first run?
Are scheduled task credentials (OAuth tokens) refreshed securely before each run without exposing them in logs?
Does the schedule system fail closed — if authentication expires, does it stop running and alert rather than running without auth?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Omar is a government security architect. Questions verify OAuth3 consent, RBAC, evidence chains, audit logs, approval gates, and fail-closed behavior for scheduled operations.

floors.persona_context: 37, Seoul, Cloud Architect at a multinational e-commerce company, Expert tech, Enterprise tier, needs Cloud Scheduler triggers to Cloud Run, solaceagi.com scheduled tasks for distributed teams, and cross-timezone team scheduling
questions: Can I trigger a Solace scheduled task via Google Cloud Scheduler sending an HTTP POST to the solaceagi.com API?
Does solaceagi.com Cloud Run wake up on schedule trigger and execute the task within the existing auto-scaling infrastructure?
Can I configure a team schedule where the same task runs at 9am local time for each team member's timezone?
Does the schedule integrate with the cloud twin — can a scheduled task spin up a cloud twin browser for execution?
Can I export schedule definitions as JSON/YAML for version control and infrastructure-as-code workflows?
Does the scheduler support cross-app orchestration — e.g., schedule a Gmail triage that triggers a Slack notification on completion?
Is there a global schedule dashboard showing all team members' schedules with timezone-normalized next-run times?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Rachel architects cloud infrastructure for a global team. Questions verify Cloud Scheduler integration, Cloud Run triggering, cross-timezone team scheduling, and infrastructure-as-code support.
